title: Indoor PCB Defect
shortDescription: The indoor unit printed circuit board has a malfunction.
fullDescription: Daikin error code A1 indicates an internal fault on the indoor unit's main control board. The PCB may have failed due to a power surge, component failure, or age.
safetyWarning: Turn off the unit at the breaker before inspecting. Mini-split units contain high voltage circuits.
symptoms: A1 error on remote or display
Unit does not respond to commands

fixSteps.title: Power Cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off the unit at the breaker for 5 minutes, then restore power.

fixSteps.title: Check Connections
fixSteps.instruction: Open the indoor unit panel and check that all connectors on the PCB are firmly seated.

fixSteps.title: Look for Damage
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the PCB for burn marks, swollen capacitors, or corrosion.

fixSteps.title: Call Professional
fixSteps.instruction: PCB replacement requires a Daikin-certified technician.

whenToCallPro: For any PCB-related error that does not clear with a power cycle.

faq.a: $300-$800 including parts and labor.

faq.q: Can a power surge cause this?
faq.a: Yes, surge protectors are recommended for mini-splits.

faq.q: How long does PCB replacement take?
faq.a: 1-3 hours for a qualified technician.

title: Drain Level System Malfunction
shortDescription: The condensate drain is blocked or the float switch is activated.
fullDescription: A3 indicates the indoor unit's condensate drain system has a problem. The drain pan is full or the drain pump has failed, triggering the float switch to shut down the unit before water overflows.
safetyWarning: Water overflow can damage ceilings, walls, and electronics. Address promptly.

fixSteps.title: Clear the Drain
fixSteps.instruction: Locate the condensate drain line and clear any blockage using a wet/dry vacuum or compressed air.

fixSteps.title: Clean the Drain Pan
fixSteps.instruction: Remove the indoor unit cover and clean the drain pan of any algae or debris.

fixSteps.title: Check Float Switch
fixSteps.instruction: Verify the float switch moves freely and is not stuck in the up position.

fixSteps.title: Reset the Unit
fixSteps.instruction: After clearing the drain, power cycle the unit to reset the error.

whenToCallPro: If the drain pump has failed or the drain pan is cracked.

faq.q: How do I prevent drain clogs?
faq.a: Pour a cup of vinegar down the drain line every few months to prevent algae growth.

faq.q: Why does my unit leak water?
faq.a: The most common cause is a clogged drain line, especially in humid environments.

faq.q: Can I clean the drain myself?
faq.a: Yes, clearing the drain line is a common DIY maintenance task.

title: Freeze Protection Activated
shortDescription: The indoor heat exchanger is freezing up.
fullDescription: A5 indicates the indoor coil temperature dropped to near-freezing levels, and the unit shut down the compressor to prevent ice damage.
safetyWarning: Let ice melt completely before restarting. Do not run a frozen unit.

fixSteps.title: Clean Filter
fixSteps.instruction: Remove and wash the indoor air filters. Let them dry before reinstalling.

fixSteps.title: Thaw
fixSteps.instruction: Run fan-only mode to melt any ice buildup.

fixSteps.title: Check Airflow
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure nothing is blocking the indoor unit intake or discharge.

fixSteps.title: Call if Recurs
fixSteps.instruction: If the error returns after cleaning filters, low refrigerant is likely.

whenToCallPro: If problem recurs after cleaning filters.

faq.a: Every 2-4 weeks during heavy use.
Yes, it can damage the compressor.
Yes, it is a common cause after filter issues are ruled out.

title: Indoor Fan Motor Error
shortDescription: The indoor fan motor has failed or is running abnormally.
fullDescription: A6 indicates a malfunction in the indoor fan motor — it may be seized, running at incorrect speed, or not responding to the control board.
safetyWarning: Turn off the unit before inspecting the fan. A stalled motor can overheat.
symptoms: Motor hums but fan does not spin

fixSteps.title: Check for Obstructions
fixSteps.instruction: Open the indoor unit and check if the fan wheel is blocked by debris.

fixSteps.title: Spin Fan Manually
fixSteps.instruction: With power off, try to spin the fan by hand. Resistance or grinding indicates bad bearings.

fixSteps.title: Power Cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Restart the unit to see if the fan starts.

fixSteps.title: Call Professional
fixSteps.instruction: Fan motor replacement needs a trained technician.

whenToCallPro: If the fan does not spin freely or the motor is making noise.

faq.a: $150-$400 for the part.

faq.q: Can I run without the fan?
faq.a: No, the coil will freeze and the compressor can be damaged.
Age, dust buildup, and restricted airflow.

title: Swing Flap Motor Error
shortDescription: The louver/swing motor is not operating correctly.
fullDescription: A7 indicates the indoor unit's air direction swing flap motor has malfunctioned. The louvers may be stuck or the motor has failed.
safetyWarning: Turn off the unit before manually adjusting louvers.

fixSteps.title: Check for Obstruction
fixSteps.instruction: Look for anything blocking the louver movement.

fixSteps.title: Gently Move Louvers
fixSteps.instruction: With power off, try gently moving the louvers by hand to check for mechanical binding.

fixSteps.title: Power Cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Restart the unit. The louvers should move to their default position.

fixSteps.title: Replace Motor
fixSteps.instruction: If the louver motor has failed, a technician can replace it.

whenToCallPro: If the louver motor needs replacement.

faq.q: Can I run the AC with louvers stuck?
faq.a: Usually yes, but air distribution will be affected.
$50-$150 for the part.
Not urgent — the unit can still cool, just with fixed air direction.

title: Leak Sensor Activated
shortDescription: The water leak sensor has detected water where it should not be.
fullDescription: C2 indicates the leak detection sensor has been triggered, usually due to condensate overflow or a drain problem.
safetyWarning: Water can damage property and create electrical hazards. Address immediately.

fixSteps.title: Clear Drain
fixSteps.instruction: Clear the condensate drain line with vacuum or compressed air.

fixSteps.title: Clean Drain Pan
fixSteps.instruction: Remove and clean the drain pan.

fixSteps.title: Check Leak Sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Dry the leak sensor and verify it resets.

fixSteps.title: Reset Unit
fixSteps.instruction: Power cycle after fixing the drain issue.

whenToCallPro: If the drain pump failed or pan is cracked.

faq.a: Regular drain line maintenance — flush with vinegar monthly.
The sensor prevents this by shutting down early.
Yes, especially in humid climates.

title: Heat Exchanger Temperature Sensor Fault (Indoor)
shortDescription: The indoor heat exchanger thermistor has failed.
fullDescription: C4 indicates the indoor coil temperature sensor is reading out of range or has an open/short circuit.
safetyWarning: Turn off unit before handling sensors.

fixSteps.title: Check Connection
fixSteps.instruction: Verify sensor wire is plugged in at PCB.

whenToCallPro: If replacement does not clear the error.

faq.a: Not safely — freeze protection will not work.
Yes, thermistors degrade over time.

title: Swing Flap Position Sensor Error
shortDescription: The louver position sensor cannot detect the flap position.
fullDescription: C7 indicates the system cannot determine the position of the air direction louver flaps due to a sensor fault.
safetyWarning: Turn off unit before inspecting louver mechanism.
symptoms: Air direction control does not work

fixSteps.title: Check Louvers
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure louvers move freely without obstruction.

fixSteps.title: Replace Sensor
fixSteps.instruction: A technician can replace the position sensor.

whenToCallPro: If louvers are mechanically free but the error persists.

faq.q: Can I still use the AC?
faq.a: Often yes, with manual air direction.
No, cooling still works.

title: Room Temperature Sensor Fault
shortDescription: The indoor room temperature sensor (thermistor) has failed.
fullDescription: C9 indicates the room temperature sensor in the indoor unit is reading out of range or has failed. The system cannot accurately measure room temperature.
safetyWarning: Turn off unit before inspecting sensors.

causes.cause: Sensor in direct sunlight or heat source

symptoms: Unit does not maintain set temperature
Runs continuously or not at all

fixSteps.title: Check Sensor Location
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure nothing is near the sensor giving it a false reading (sunlight, lamp, etc).

fixSteps.title: Test Sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Measure resistance and compare to spec.

whenToCallPro: If replacement does not resolve the issue.

faq.a: Age and environmental factors.

faq.q: Can heat from a lamp affect it?
faq.a: Yes, external heat sources near the sensor cause false readings.

title: Safety Device Activated (Outdoor)
shortDescription: A safety protection device on the outdoor unit has activated.
fullDescription: E0 indicates a general safety device activation on the outdoor unit. This is a broad code that may indicate overheating, overcurrent, or other safety conditions.
safetyWarning: Turn off the system. Multiple safety conditions may require professional diagnosis.

fixSteps.title: Clean Outdoor Coil
fixSteps.instruction: Hose down the outdoor coil from inside out.

fixSteps.title: Check Airflow
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure 2+ feet clearance around the outdoor unit.

fixSteps.title: Call Professional
fixSteps.instruction: If error returns, professional diagnosis is needed.

whenToCallPro: If cleaning does not resolve the issue.

faq.a: It can be — it indicates a safety device activated.
Includes high pressure switch, overcurrent protection, and thermal overload.
Call a professional — may be a refrigerant issue.

title: Outdoor PCB Error
shortDescription: The outdoor unit control board has malfunctioned.
fullDescription: E1 indicates an internal fault on the outdoor unit's PCB, similar to A1 but for the outdoor unit.
safetyWarning: Turn off at the breaker. High voltage present in outdoor unit.
symptoms: Indoor unit may run fan only

fixSteps.title: Power Cycle
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off at breaker for 5 minutes.

fixSteps.title: Visual Inspect
fixSteps.instruction: Look for visible damage on outdoor PCB.

whenToCallPro: If power cycling does not clear.

faq.a: $200-$600 for the part.
Yes, use a surge protector.
Check your Daikin warranty — PCBs may be covered for up to 5 years.

title: High Pressure Switch Activated
shortDescription: The outdoor high pressure safety switch has tripped.
fullDescription: E3 indicates excessive refrigerant pressure on the high side, triggering the safety switch.
safetyWarning: Do not attempt to adjust refrigerant. High pressure systems are dangerous.

fixSteps.title: Clear Obstruction
fixSteps.instruction: Remove anything blocking the outdoor unit.

whenToCallPro: If cleaning does not resolve.

faq.a: Usually auto-resets after pressure drops.
Yes, hot days stress the condenser.
Yes, plants blocking the outdoor unit restrict airflow.

title: Compressor Overcurrent
shortDescription: The compressor is drawing excessive current.
fullDescription: E5 indicates the compressor motor is exceeding its rated current draw.
safetyWarning: Overcurrent can cause fire. Turn off the system at the breaker immediately.
whenToCallPro: After one failed restart.

faq.a: Possibly, but it could also be a capacitor or voltage issue.
Yes, overcurrent is a fire risk.

title: Outdoor Fan Motor Error
shortDescription: The outdoor fan motor has malfunctioned.
fullDescription: E7 indicates the outdoor fan motor is not running or running abnormally.
safetyWarning: Turn off unit. A non-functional outdoor fan causes rapid pressure buildup.

fixSteps.title: Check Fan Blade
fixSteps.instruction: With power off, check if blade spins freely and is not blocked.

whenToCallPro: If fan does not spin freely.

faq.a: No, the unit will overheat immediately.
10-15 years typically.

title: Electronic Expansion Valve Error
shortDescription: The electronic expansion valve is not operating correctly.
fullDescription: E9 indicates the electronic expansion valve (EEV) that controls refrigerant flow has malfunctioned.
safetyWarning: EEV is a precision component. Do not attempt adjustment.

causes.cause: Wiring issue between PCB and EEV

symptoms: Refrigerant lines unusually cold or warm

fixSteps.title: Check Wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect EEV wiring at the outdoor unit board.

fixSteps.title: Listen for EEV
fixSteps.instruction: On startup, the EEV should make a buzzing/stepping sound. Silence indicates failure.

fixSteps.title: Call Professional
fixSteps.instruction: EEV replacement requires recovery and recharge of refrigerant.

whenToCallPro: For any EEV-related code.

faq.a: An electronically controlled valve that precisely regulates refrigerant flow.
$100-$300 for the part; $300-$800 total.

faq.q: Can the system run with a bad EEV?
faq.a: It will run poorly with incorrect refrigerant flow.

title: Discharge Pipe Temperature Too High
shortDescription: Compressor discharge line temperature exceeded the safe limit.
fullDescription: F3 indicates the temperature at the compressor discharge pipe is dangerously high, typically from low refrigerant or restricted airflow.
safetyWarning: Turn off the system to protect the compressor from heat damage.
whenToCallPro: If cleaning does not help.

faq.a: Discharge temps above 250F are dangerous for the compressor.
Often yes.
Yes, continued operation can damage the compressor.

title: High Pressure Detected (Abnormal)
shortDescription: Abnormally high pressure in the refrigerant system.
fullDescription: F6 indicates the system has detected high pressure through sensor readings rather than the mechanical high pressure switch. This is an early warning before the mechanical switch trips.
safetyWarning: Turn off the system. Do not tamper with refrigerant connections.

causes.cause: Fan not running at full speed

fixSteps.title: Check Fan
fixSteps.instruction: Outdoor fan should run at full speed.

whenToCallPro: If cleaning and clearance do not resolve.

faq.a: F6 is sensor-based detection; E3 is the mechanical switch. F6 is the earlier warning.
Often yes, especially in dusty or leafy environments.
At least 2-3 times per year in dirty environments.

title: Compressor Sensor Error
shortDescription: The compressor temperature sensor has failed.
fullDescription: H0 indicates the compressor temperature thermistor on the outdoor unit has failed or is reading out of range.
safetyWarning: Turn off the system before inspecting outdoor unit components.

fixSteps.title: Check Connection
fixSteps.instruction: Verify sensor is plugged in at outdoor PCB.

whenToCallPro: If replacement does not clear error.

faq.a: Yes — compressor temperature protection depends on this sensor.
Sensors degrade with age and heat exposure.

title: Compressor Motor Position Detection Error
shortDescription: The inverter cannot detect the compressor motor rotor position.
fullDescription: H6 indicates the outdoor unit's inverter drive cannot detect the position of the compressor motor, which is needed for speed control.
safetyWarning: Inverter components carry high voltage. Professional service required.

fixSteps.title: Check Wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Professional check of compressor wiring at the inverter board.

whenToCallPro: Immediately — this requires professional diagnosis.

faq.a: It can be, but sometimes the inverter board is the issue.

faq.q: How much for an inverter board?
faq.a: $300-$800 installed.

title: Outdoor Air Temperature Sensor Fault
shortDescription: The outdoor ambient temperature sensor has failed.
fullDescription: H9 indicates the outdoor air temperature thermistor is reading incorrectly or has failed.
safetyWarning: Turn off outdoor unit before inspecting.
symptoms: System may not operate in certain modes
May affect defrost in heat pump mode

fixSteps.title: Check Sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Find and inspect the outdoor ambient sensor.

whenToCallPro: If replacement does not help.

faq.a: $10-$30 for sensor.
Yes, in heat pump mode.
Sensors exposed to weather degrade over time.

title: Pressure Sensor Error
shortDescription: The refrigerant pressure sensor is reading incorrectly.
fullDescription: J1 indicates a pressure sensor (transducer) on the outdoor unit has failed or is reading out of range.
safetyWarning: Do not tamper with refrigerant system components.

fixSteps.title: Check Wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect sensor wiring at outdoor PCB.

fixSteps.title: Test Sensor
fixSteps.instruction: Professional testing of pressure transducer signal.

whenToCallPro: For any pressure sensor code.

faq.a: The system cannot operate safely without accurate pressure readings.
Not recommended until repaired.

title: Discharge Pipe Temperature Sensor Fault
shortDescription: The compressor discharge pipe temperature sensor has failed.
fullDescription: J3 indicates the thermistor measuring the compressor discharge line temperature is faulty.
safetyWarning: Turn off the unit before inspecting sensors.
symptoms: System may run in safe mode
whenToCallPro: If replacement does not clear.

faq.a: It prevents the compressor from overheating.
Yes — without this sensor, the compressor has no overheat protection.

title: Suction Pipe Temperature Sensor Fault
shortDescription: The suction pipe temperature sensor on the outdoor unit has failed.
fullDescription: J9 indicates the thermistor on the compressor suction line is reading out of range.
safetyWarning: Professional repair required for outdoor unit sensors.
whenToCallPro: If replacement does not fix it.

faq.a: Monitors refrigerant temperature returning to the compressor.
Yes, helps protect the compressor from liquid return.

title: Inverter PCB Error
shortDescription: The inverter power control board has an internal error.
fullDescription: L1 indicates a fault in the inverter PCB that controls compressor speed. This is a critical component failure.
safetyWarning: High voltage present. Do not open the outdoor unit electrical compartment.

fixSteps.title: Check for Recurrence
fixSteps.instruction: If error returns immediately, the board has failed.

fixSteps.title: Do Not Attempt DIY
fixSteps.instruction: Inverter repairs involve high voltage DC and are dangerous.

fixSteps.title: Call Professional
fixSteps.instruction: Inverter board replacement by Daikin-certified tech.

whenToCallPro: Immediately.

faq.q: Can the old board be repaired?
faq.a: Sometimes, but replacement is more reliable.
Daikin compressor/inverter warranties can be 5-12 years.

title: Compressor Overcurrent at Startup (Instantaneous)
shortDescription: The compressor drew excessive current at the moment of startup.
fullDescription: L5 indicates an instantaneous overcurrent was detected when the compressor tried to start, before reaching running speed.
safetyWarning: Do not keep restarting. Excessive starting current can damage the inverter.

causes.cause: Wiring fault between inverter and compressor

symptoms: Compressor attempts to start then stops

fixSteps.title: One Retry
fixSteps.instruction: Restart once. If L5 returns, stop.

fixSteps.title: Do Not Keep Trying
fixSteps.instruction: Repeated starts with overcurrent damage the inverter.

whenToCallPro: After one failed restart.

faq.a: Not necessarily — could be an inverter issue.

faq.q: Can I add a hard start?
faq.a: Daikin inverter systems do not use traditional hard-start kits.

title: Compressor Start Failure
shortDescription: The compressor failed to reach operating speed.
fullDescription: L9 indicates the compressor attempted to start but could not reach its target speed within the allowed time.
safetyWarning: Professional diagnosis required. Do not attempt repeated restarts.

fixSteps.title: Check Voltage
fixSteps.instruction: Verify power supply voltage is correct.

whenToCallPro: After one failed restart.

faq.a: Mechanical wear, low charge, or inverter issues.
Often yes — may be a charge or inverter issue rather than compressor failure.
Check your Daikin warranty for compressor coverage.

title: Refrigerant Shortage Detected
shortDescription: The system has detected low refrigerant charge.
fullDescription: P0 indicates the Daikin mini-split has detected that the refrigerant charge is below the minimum operating level, typically from a leak.
safetyWarning: Only EPA-certified technicians should handle refrigerant.

fixSteps.title: Check for Leaks
fixSteps.instruction: Look for oil stains at flare connections.

whenToCallPro: Immediately for any refrigerant shortage.

faq.a: No, the leak must be found and repaired.
Not recommended — damages the compressor.

title: Inverter Fin Temperature Sensor Fault
shortDescription: The temperature sensor on the inverter heat sink has failed.
fullDescription: P4 indicates the thermistor monitoring the inverter board heat sink temperature is faulty. This sensor protects the inverter from overheating.
safetyWarning: Inverter components are high voltage. Professional service required.

fixSteps.title: Check for Recurrence
fixSteps.instruction: If error returns, sensor or board has failed.

fixSteps.title: Clean Outdoor Unit
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure airflow to the inverter area is not blocked.

whenToCallPro: If power cycling does not clear.

faq.a: Monitors inverter heat sink temp to prevent overheating.
Yes, without it the inverter can overheat and fail.

title: Refrigerant Shortage (System Level)
shortDescription: System-level low refrigerant detection.
fullDescription: U0 is another refrigerant shortage indicator, typically found on multi-zone Daikin systems.
safetyWarning: Turn off system. EPA-certified technician required for refrigerant.

fixSteps.title: Inspect Connections
fixSteps.instruction: Look for oil at flare fittings.

fixSteps.title: Note Affected Zones
fixSteps.instruction: Record which zones are affected for the technician.

whenToCallPro: Immediately.

faq.a: U0 is system-level detection on multi-zone systems; P0 is unit-level.
Flare connections are common leak points on multi-zone installations.

title: Power Supply Voltage Abnormal
shortDescription: Supply voltage to the outdoor unit is outside normal range.
fullDescription: U2 indicates the voltage supplied to the Daikin outdoor unit is too high or too low.
safetyWarning: Do not modify electrical connections.

fixSteps.title: Wait
fixSteps.instruction: Wait 30 minutes for voltage to stabilize.

fixSteps.title: Call Electrician
fixSteps.instruction: Persistent voltage issues need panel inspection.

whenToCallPro: If error recurs.

faq.a: Yes, they cause U2.
Yes, single occurrence is usually fine.
Recommended for mini-splits.

title: Communication Error Between Indoor and Outdoor Units
shortDescription: The indoor and outdoor units have lost communication.
fullDescription: U4 is one of the most common Daikin mini-split errors. It indicates the communication link between indoor and outdoor units has been broken.
safetyWarning: Turn off both units before checking wiring.

causes.cause: Power supply issue to one unit

symptoms: One unit runs, other does not

fixSteps.title: Power Cycle Both
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off both units at their breakers for 5 minutes, then restart.

fixSteps.title: Check Communication Wire
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect the 3-wire or signal cable between indoor and outdoor units for damage or loose connections.

fixSteps.title: Verify Both Have Power
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure both indoor and outdoor breakers are on.

fixSteps.title: Call Professional
fixSteps.instruction: If power cycling and wire checks fail, a PCB may need replacement.

whenToCallPro: If power cycling does not restore communication.

faq.a: Yes, it is one of the most frequent Daikin error codes, often from loose connections or power issues.
Very common after outages. Power cycling both units usually fixes it.
Communication cables can be damaged by rodents, weather, or construction.

title: Communication Error in Multi-System
shortDescription: Communication failure between units in a multi-zone system.
fullDescription: U9 indicates a communication error between multiple indoor units or between indoor units and the branch box in a multi-zone Daikin system.
safetyWarning: Multi-zone system wiring is complex. Professional service recommended.

causes.cause: Loose communication wire at branch box

symptoms: One or more zones not working

fixSteps.title: Power Cycle All
fixSteps.instruction: Turn off all units and the outdoor unit for 5 minutes.

fixSteps.title: Check Branch Box Wiring
fixSteps.instruction: Inspect all connections at the branch box if accessible.

fixSteps.title: Note Affected Zones
fixSteps.instruction: Record which zones have errors for the technician.

fixSteps.title: Call Daikin Dealer
fixSteps.instruction: Multi-zone communication issues often need dealer diagnostic tools.

whenToCallPro: If power cycling does not restore all zones.

faq.q: Is this just one zone or all?
faq.a: May affect one or more zones depending on the communication path.
A distribution component that routes refrigerant and communication between the outdoor unit and multiple indoor units.
$200-$800 depending on the issue.

title: Compressor Locked
shortDescription: The compressor motor is mechanically locked and cannot rotate.
fullDescription: E2 indicates the compressor rotor is mechanically stuck or the motor windings have failed, preventing rotation.
safetyWarning: Turn off the system. A locked compressor draws dangerous current.
whenToCallPro: After one failed restart.

faq.a: Not necessarily — could be inverter or wiring.

title: Defrost Mode Active
shortDescription: The heat pump is in defrost mode — this is normal during heating season.
fullDescription: H1 is not an error but a status indicator showing the heat pump outdoor unit is in defrost mode to melt ice from the coil. The indoor unit temporarily stops heating during defrost.
safetyWarning: No safety concern — this is normal heat pump operation.

causes.cause: Frequent defrost may indicate low refrigerant
Outdoor coil dirty causing excessive ice
Defrost sensor issue if too frequent

fixSteps.title: Wait
fixSteps.instruction: Defrost normally takes 5-15 minutes. Heat will resume automatically.

faq.q: Why does the indoor unit blow cold?

title: High Pressure Switch Fault
shortDescription: The high pressure switch circuit is abnormal.
fullDescription: H3 indicates the high pressure switch circuit on the outdoor unit has an issue — either the switch itself or its wiring.
safetyWarning: High pressure systems can be dangerous. Professional service required.

fixSteps.title: Clean Outdoor Coil
fixSteps.instruction: Rule out actual high pressure by cleaning coils.

title: Low Pressure Switch Fault
shortDescription: The low pressure switch circuit has an issue.
fullDescription: H4 indicates a fault in the low pressure switch circuit — either the switch or its wiring.
safetyWarning: Professional service required for pressure switch work.

fixSteps.title: Check for Ice
fixSteps.instruction: Look for ice on refrigerant lines.

whenToCallPro: If power cycling does not resolve.

title: Electrical Component Temperature Too High
shortDescription: The temperature of electrical components in the outdoor unit is too high.
fullDescription: L3 indicates overheating of the power transistors or other electrical components on the inverter board, usually from poor ventilation.
safetyWarning: Overheating components can cause fire. Turn off immediately.

causes.cause: Dirty heat sink on inverter board
Failed cooling fan inside outdoor unit

symptoms: Hot to touch near electrical compartment

fixSteps.title: Clear Obstructions
fixSteps.instruction: Ensure 2+ feet of clearance on all sides of the outdoor unit.

fixSteps.title: Clean Outdoor Unit
fixSteps.instruction: Clean the coils and ensure airflow is not restricted.

fixSteps.title: Wait to Cool
fixSteps.instruction: Let the unit cool for 30 minutes before restarting.

fixSteps.title: Call Professional
fixSteps.instruction: If error returns, the heat sink or internal fan may need service.

whenToCallPro: If clearing obstructions and cleaning does not resolve.

faq.a: Hot weather combined with poor ventilation can trigger this.
Yes, units in direct sun or enclosed spaces are more prone.
Keep the outdoor unit in a well-ventilated location with clearance.

title: Power Supply Phase Error
shortDescription: The power supply phase or voltage is abnormal for the outdoor unit.
fullDescription: P1 indicates a phase detection error or power supply abnormality on three-phase Daikin outdoor units.
safetyWarning: Electrical supply issues can damage equipment. Contact electrician if recurrent.

fixSteps.title: Check Power
fixSteps.instruction: Verify all three phases are present at the disconnect.

fixSteps.title: Check Connections
fixSteps.instruction: Tighten all power connections at disconnect and outdoor unit.

fixSteps.title: Call Electrician
fixSteps.instruction: Phase loss or imbalance needs electrical service.

whenToCallPro: For any phase-related error on 3-phase systems.

faq.a: Yes, single-phase units do not show P1.
One of the three power phases is missing, often from a blown fuse or utility issue.
Only if you have a multimeter and understand 3-phase power.

title: DC Bus Voltage Error
shortDescription: The DC bus voltage in the inverter is abnormal.
fullDescription: P3 indicates the DC voltage converted from AC power within the inverter circuit is out of the normal operating range, preventing the compressor from operating.
safetyWarning: High voltage DC is present in the inverter. Do not open the outdoor unit electrical compartment.

fixSteps.title: Wait
fixSteps.instruction: Wait 30 minutes for voltage to stabilize.

whenToCallPro: If power cycling does not resolve.

faq.a: The converted DC power that drives the inverter compressor.
Often yes — voltage fluctuations can trigger it.
$300-$800 installed.
